reforms.description: The Government adopted a series of measures aimed at reducing the overall balance of payments surplus and stimulating economic activity, including a reduction in interest rates and acceleration of public investment expenditure.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The measures are designed to stimulate economic activity and improve domestic demand, which can enhance productivity and investment.
reforms.status_evidence: The Government adopted a series of measures aimed at reducing the overall balance of payments surplus and at stimulating economic activity.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform package represents a significant shift in monetary policy and public investment strategy, aimed at addressing economic challenges and stimulating growth.
reforms.source_quote: On 23rd May 1972, the Government adopted a series of measures (the '7-point programme') aimed at reducing the overall balance of payments surplus and at stimulating economic activity.
